test_host: mini2 (Mac mini M4, 16 GB, clean) over Thunderbolt SSH tunnels; camelid serve + pinned llama.cpp both on mini2

model.head_dim_note: EXPLICIT: 128 != embedding/head_count (2560/32=80); same path proven on 0.6B

result_note: 2 of 3 standard probes (capital-of-France, say-hello) are token-AND-text-identical at 1/5/50. The 'Name a primary color.' probe diverges at the FIRST token: llama.cpp ranks 'The' (logprob -0.818) over 'Red' (-1.156) by only 0.34 logit (genuine model uncertainty); camelid's f32 path flips that near-tie to 'Red'. Three additional confident probes (incl. 'What is 2 plus 2?') were token-identical at 50 tokens, confirming the forward is correct and the divergence is the documented near-tie frontier, not a defect.
